changes.value: nit: ToolConfigWatcher doc comment is misplaced above Default impl, not the struct

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1 +1 @@
-swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tool_config.rs:162-180

The doc comment explaining `ToolConfigWatcher` and its `check_and_reload` method is attached to the `impl Default for ToolConfigWatcher` block rather than the `struct ToolConfigWatcher` declaration that follows it:

```rust
/// Watches tool config files for changes...  ŌåÉ doc on the Default impl
impl Default for ToolConfigWatcher {
    fn default() -> Self { Self::new() }
}

pub struct ToolConfigWatcher {  ŌåÉ struct has no doc comment
    ...
}
```

This means `cargo doc` will not render the explanation on the struct page ŌĆö it will appear on the `Default` impl instead. All public items must have doc comments directly on the item itself.

Suggestion: Move the doc comment to the `struct ToolConfigWatcher` line and add a minimal `/// Create a default watcher.` to the `Default` impl if desired.

Verification: `cargo doc --open` shows the description on the struct, not the impl.

#review-finding
\ No newline at end of file
